definition: goggles are a special pair of glasses that you wear over your eyes to keep them safe from things like dust, wind, snow, water, etc.
example: You must wear goggles when you ski to protect your eyes from the snow and wind.

definition: a helmet is a hard hat you wear to protect your head
example: My bike helmet is uncomfortable to wear, but I have to wear it when cycling.

definition: a lifejacket is a special vest you wear, usually over clothes or a swimsuit, that helps you to float in water
example: When Paolo fell into the water, she was glad she was wearing a lifejacket to help her float.

definition: a wetsuit is a special suit made of rubber that is right on your body and keeps you warm in water
example: Olivier always wears his wetsuit when he goes surfing in the morning.

definition: something that is complicated has many parts to it and is difficult to understand
example: This is a complicated math problem, but I’m sure I can work out the right answer.

definition: safety is the condition of being safe from danger
example: The children were led to a place of safety.

definition: something that is waterproof stops water from going through or into it
example: Kensu’s new watch is waterproof, so he can wear it while swimming.

definition: something that is average is usual or common
example: According to research, the average person watches nearly three hours of TV daily.

definition: when two or more things range from each other, they have a higher and a lower number to show the difference between them
example: The time it takes to fly from Tel Aviv to Beijing ranges from seven and a half to 17 hours.

definition: something that is stressful makes you feel worried so you can’t relax
example: Yannick deals with stressful situations very well.

definition: you snowboard by standing with both feet on a long board that lets you move over snow
example: Dorien likes to snowboard on weekends during the winter when the snow is thick.

definition: you paddleboard by standing on a long board and using a special stick to move over water
example: Zoe lives next to a lake, so she paddleboards across the water every morning.

definition: when you land, you arrive at a place
example: The plane will land in Copenhagen on time.

definition: backpacking is when you travel in a cheap way and usually camp or stay in hostels
example: Voula and Gregori are going backpacking in Laos next month.

definition: if you do something stupidly, you do it in a silly way
example: We stupidly locked the keys in the car.

definition: something that is alternative is different to the first choice
example: As some of the roads are blocked, drivers must use an alternative route.

definition: a trail is a path through a forest or on a mountain or similar place that people walk, hike, or run on
example: This hiking trail goes through a forest and has some hills around it.

definition: somewhere that is rural is not in a town or a city
example: Leila’s family moved from New Delhi to a rural town.

definition: something that is rough is not smooth to touch
example: The skin on her hands was hard and rough.

definition: something that is narrow is thin and doesn’t have much space on the sides
example: The door was too narrow to carry the bed through it.

definition: a sack is a large bag made of paper, plastic, or cloth that is used to carry or keep things in
example: There’s a sack of potatoes on the floor next to the stove.

definition: a destination is the place that you are going to
example: Phuket is a popular destination for people who want to go to the beach on vacation.

definition: a way of life is how you choose to live your life either as an individual or in a group
example: YeSeong adapted easily to the Finnish way of life.

definition: scenery is the natural world that you see around you, especially in the countryside
example: The village has beautiful scenery that you’ll love.

definition: if you advise someone, you tell them what you think they should do
example: The doctor advised Kangdae to get more sleep.

definition: a nest is like a house that birds build with straw, feathers, and other things they find on the ground
example: There are four eggs in the bird’s nest.

definition: the coast is the land that is next to or very close to the ocean
example: Ayoub drove along the coast, with beautiful views of the ocean.

definition: a birdwatcher is someone who enjoys looking at birds in a natural setting and identifying the type of birds they see
example: The birdwatcher saw many eagles yesterday and took lots of photos.

definition: if a baby bird or animal hatches, it breaks open its eggshell and is born
example: It was interesting to watch the baby birds hatch from their eggs.

definition: if you manage to do something, you are successful in doing it
example: How do you manage to keep your house so clean with three small children?

definition: a border is the line that says where one country ends and another country starts
example: Oleg lives in Estonia but near the border of Latvia.

definition: when you board a train, bus, boat, or airplane, you get inside it to travel on it
example: You can board the plane at 8:45 p.m.

definition: a line is a row of people or vehicles that are waiting in a line to do something or go somewhere
example: We got into the line to buy our tickets for the show.

definition: when you take off, the plane you are traveling on leaves the ground and starts to fly
example: Catalina’s ears always hurt from the extra pressure when a plane takes off.

definition: if someone searches your bag, usually before traveling, they look in it to see if you have anything illegal or dangerous inside it
example: When we arrived at the airport, we had to open our bags for the staff to search them.

definition: if you are nervous about something, you feel scared
example: Zied is nervous about his driving exam tomorrow, but I’m sure he’ll pass.

definition: if you intend to do something, you have a plan to do it
example: I intend to go to bed early tonight.

definition: if you can’t afford something, you don’t have enough money to buy it
example: I can’t afford to buy a new computer right now.

definition: if you say you will take time off from something, you mean you won’t do it for a set period of time take time off, have time off
example: I hope to take time off work for a week or two to visit my parents.

definition: a timetable is a list of times that a type of transportation follows
example: The bus timetable has changed, so check it before you leave the house.

definition: if something is incredible, it is very good or exciting
example: The view of the mountains from the house is incredible.

definition: if something or someone is annoying, it/they bother/s you and make/s you unable to enjoy what you’re doing
example: My neighbor’s loud music is so annoying that I can’t sleep.

definition: if something is infuriating, it makes you very, very angry
example: The constant noise from the machine was infuriating.

definition: if something is incomprehensible, it is impossible to understand it
example: The application form was so complicated that is was incomprehensible.

definition: if you don’t catch what someone says, you don’t hear it
example: Please could you repeat that. I didn’t catch it.

definition: a wake-up call is a phone call the reception desk at a hotel makes to you to wake you up in the morning
example: Please ask for a wake-up call at the hotel so you don’t oversleep.

definition: a schedule is a list of things and the time they will happen
example: Let me check my schedule to see if I can meet with you then.

definition: roller-skating is an activity you do on a piece of hard ground in special shoes that have four small wheels on the bottom of them
example: Roller-skating used to be a popular activity to do in the 1990s.

definition: a bunk is a bed in a hostel that is cheap and usually in a shared room with other people
example: My bunk in the hostel was in a noisy room, with many people.

definition: if something is appropriate, it is right for a specific use or time
example: Jeans are not appropriate for a formal party in many countries.
